title: clickable-diagram widget params
description: A shared widget module driving multiple category-theory diagram widgets. Dispatches on the `interaction` field between two modes: 'readout-only' (select + DATA-dictionary readout; drives w-univ and w-mon) and 'proof-stepper' (select + nav buttons + SVG diagram of objects + morphisms; drives w-proof). Portable consumers can ignore the *Literal artifact fields and render from the structured `cases` / `objects` / `morphisms` data; the literals exist so renderScript can byte-match the legacy inline source.

cases.description: Portable data: keys are option values; each entry is an object of string fields consumed by the template.

dataLiteral.description: ARTIFACT. Verbatim JS object-literal text for `const DATA = <here>;` in the driving IIFE. Preserves the original source's manual alignment (e.g. irregular spacing after keys). A portable consumer regenerates from `cases` and ignores this.

templateLiteral.description: ARTIFACT. Verbatim JS text for the right-hand side of `out.textContent = <here>;` — the backtick-literal chain that formats the readout. Preserves original newlines and indentation.

svgWidthAttr.description: ARTIFACT. Raw width attr value — may be a % or px literal.

svgStyleAttr.description: ARTIFACT. Raw style attr value for the <svg>.

outputLogStyleAttr.description: ARTIFACT. Raw style attr value for the log readout div.

initialKey.description: Option value the IIFE starts on (`let key = '…'`).

cases.description: Portable data: keys are option values; each entry carries { title, intro, nodes, given, steps } describing one proof.

proofsLiteral.description: ARTIFACT. Verbatim JS object-literal text for `const PROOFS = <here>;`. Preserves the original source's alignment (e.g. `x: 130, y:  80` with double space) and inline comments.
